creator: kkockro@web.de
text:
Hi,

with this driver update we have reproducible kernel panics and controller 
resets.

Our config:

/c0 Driver Version = 3.70.05.001
/c0 Model = 9690SA-4I
/c0 Available Memory = 448MB
/c0 Firmware Version = FH9X 4.10.00.007
/c0 Bios Version = BE9X 4.08.00.002                                                                                                                                                                                              
/c0 Boot Loader Version = BL9X 3.08.00.001                                                                                                                                                                                       
/c0 Serial Number = L340525A0010159

u0 RAID1 with WDC WD3202ABYS

u1 - u14 Single Drives with ST32000644NS

u1 - u14 are working in a zfs raidz2. If i start massive writing on this 
dataset, this errors are shown:

twa0: ERROR: (0x05: 0x2018): Passthru reuqest timed out!: request = 
0xffffff80007476a0
twa0: INFO: (0x16: 0x1108): Resetting controller...:
twa0: INFO: (0x04: 0x0063): Enclosure added: encl=0
twa0: INFO: (0x04: 0x0001): Controller reset occured: resets=1
twa0: INFO: (0x16: 0x1107): Controller reset done!:
twa0: ERROR: (0x05: 0x201A): Firmware passthru failed!: error = 60

Then we have to hard reboot the server. System is FreeBSD8-STABLE amd64. With 
the earlier drivers, we had no problems!

If you need more informations, let me know.

Greets and Thanks,
Kai
